paragraphs.text: 图
Léon McGregor

section_path: EU Consultation Response - Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: The proposed digital fairness act goals are good, and in line with EU citizens rights, and business responsibilities. All of the proposed fairness principles should be investigated and legislated. In this document I will offer additional commentary of related issues that the DFA should consider.

evidence_quotes.quote: A dark pattern when not clearly and obviously disclosing advertising/sponsors/paid placement of content within a set of normal content.

paragraphs.text: A dark pattern when not clearly and obviously disclosing advertising/sponsors/paid placement of content within a set of normal content. This should be considered a dark pattern if done on all websites and apps, including publishers and app operators, not just social influencers.

evidence_quotes.quote: Google changing design of ads over time to make it less easy to distinguish from regular search results.
A news blog posting a sponsored article with only a minor sentence disclosing this buried within the post.

paragraphs.text: Google changing design of ads over time to make it less easy to distinguish from regular search results. A news blog posting a sponsored article with only a minor sentence disclosing this buried within the post.

evidence_quotes.quote: Requiring obvious disclosure

paragraphs.text: Requiring obvious disclosure would have social impact, making it easier for people to trust the web as they use it.
Visual of changes made making google ads more difficult to spot
Backtracking of changes making ads difficult to identify

evidence_quotes.quote: sites and software often treat responses differently, by accepting a confirmatory "yes" as a once-and-done response, but instead taking a "no" as consent to be asked at a later date

paragraphs.text: Many interactions with software require consent in order to function. A user must consent before nonfunctional tracking cookies can be placed. A user must consent before notifications can be enabled. However, sites and software often treat responses differently, by accepting a confirmatory "yes" as a once-and-done response, but instead taking a "no" as consent to be asked at a later date,

evidence_quotes.quote: sometimes not even offering a “no”

paragraphs.text: sometimes not even offering a “no”. This may be related to “nagging”, but given the importance of consent, should be investigated as its own issue.

evidence_quotes.quote: the only choices are a confirmatory “yes” and a negative “maybe later”
The only way to actually decline consent might be to find a small “x” hidden on a message, and even then this might be taken as the same meaning as “maybe later”.

paragraphs.text: An operating system wants to ask for permission to enable a cloud service, and the only choices are a confirmatory “yes” and a negative “maybe later”, where the user is effectively consenting to be asked repeatedly. The only way to actually decline consent might be to find a small “x” hidden on a message, and even then this might be taken as the same meaning as “maybe later”.

evidence_quotes.quote: clear and obvious “no” response available

paragraphs.text: Forcing developers of software to treat consent responses the same way would ensure that for every “yes” consent there is a clear and obvious “no” response available also, ensuring that consumers have choice to make informed consent.
Summary of the UI trend by a designer

evidence_quotes.quote: The use of arbitrary restrictions that prevent consumers from having free choice, under the guise of security.

paragraphs.text: The use of arbitrary restrictions that prevent consumers from having free choice, under the guise of security.

evidence_quotes.quote: An application bans users with custom android installations from installing and using their apps, using a service like google play device attestation.

paragraphs.text: An application bans users with custom android installations from installing and using their apps, using a service like google play device attestation.

This would have economic impact, making it easier to develop, launch, and use devices without needing to go through ecosystem gatekeepers. It would also have a social impact, offering people more choice to use private systems without needing to give up access to apps and services. It would also have environmental impact, as installing custom operating systems uninhibited by security theatre can extend the life of hardware.
A popular alternative android operating system has lengthy instructions for users affected by this
An EU identity app project drew concern from users over this issue
D. Complex legalese language

evidence_quotes.quote: The use of the language of “licenses” as a legalese way to take ownership away from consumers.
Replacing this with the terminology of “licenses” allows sellers to terminate access for any reason at any time.

paragraphs.text: The use of the language of “licenses” as a legalese way to take ownership away from consumers. If a consumer “buys” or “purchases” a product, they expect to own a copy and be able to use it as they wish for any personal purpose as long as they want. Replacing this with the terminology of “licenses” allows sellers to terminate access for any reason at any time. Expecting the average consumer to understand the difference is unreasonable.

evidence_quotes.quote: using the language of “license” to prevent the consumer from legally owning what they have purchased.

paragraphs.text: Selling a video game or film within a vendor ecosystem, and using the language of “license” to prevent the consumer from legally owning what they have purchased.
This would have social impact as it would empower consumers to take more ownership of what they are purchasing.

evidence_quotes.quote: If a consumer is clicking “buy” on a digital product, that they could reasonably expect to own that copy.

paragraphs.text: EU consumer rights often rely on the concept of a consumer’s “reasonable expectation”. If a consumer is clicking “buy” on a digital product, that they could reasonably expect to own that copy.

evidence_quotes.quote: recommender systems
an algorithmic mechanism designed in accordance with user groups
a community of users they trust

paragraphs.text: Allowing communities of service users to build their own recommender systems. These could be done via human curation, or using an algorithmic mechanism designed in accordance with user groups. Users could then choose between default (chronological) orderings and recommendations, vendor-provided recommendation systems, or systems developed by a community of users they trust. Human curated lists of recommendations have existed on social services for a long time, allowing communities to develop their own algorithmic recommender is a logical evolution of this.

evidence_quotes.quote: recommendation system that steers child users away from harmful content or disinformation
parents could subscribe to this without needing the vendor’s approval.

paragraphs.text: A child safety organisation could develop a recommendation system that steers child users away from harmful content or disinformation, and parents could subscribe to this without needing the vendor’s approval.
Impact

evidence_quotes.quote: building their own recommendation algorithms
people could subscribe to

paragraphs.text: This would have social impact, offering more protection for vulnerable groups, and empower service users to make their own choices. It could also have an economic impact if businesses were able to compete on building their own recommendation algorithms people could subscribe to.
Streaming services like spotify allow customers to collaborate, create and share playlists of related content
Bluesky “starter packs” built buy the community as an alternative to algorithm-driven recommendations

evidence_quotes.quote: end of life plan to ensure that once the publisher ends support, the games remain playable and are not taken away from consumers who have purchased them.
An end of life plan would involve offering sufficient documentation and configuration for a game to allow it to run when support ends.

paragraphs.text: Games that require publisher support must have an end of life plan to ensure that once the publisher ends support, the games remain playable and are not taken away from consumers who have purchased them. An end of life plan would involve offering sufficient documentation and configuration for a game to allow it to run when support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: A competitive multiplayer game that uses matchmaking

paragraphs.text: A competitive multiplayer game that uses matchmaking requires publisher support, and once that ends a plan is enacted that allows owners of the game to directly connect to each other, without the need for support. This policy is a part of European Citizens Initiative .

evidence_quotes.quote: out-of-support videogames.

paragraphs.text: This would bring social impact, preserving the cultural value of out-of-support videogames. It could also give economic value, as the resources invested into these games would not be lost when support ends, and third parties could operate in a tertiary market offering support to officially unsupported games.

evidence_quotes.quote: A list of affected games

paragraphs.text: A list of affected games that could have been saved had an End of Life plan been prepared
B. Allow bypassing anti-cheat for non-competitive play

evidence_quotes.quote: Games that have anti-cheat systems should allow the player to play the game without installing these.
Some anti cheat systems are considered invasive spyware, requiring high level permissions on a system, regardless if the player intends to engage in competitive play or not.

paragraphs.text: Games that have anti-cheat systems should allow the player to play the game without installing these. Anti-Cheat systems are a big barrier in game portability for systems such as Linux, and so giving players the choice to not use these would open up these games to a wider range of operating systems and allow the industry to develop and sell their own platforms using Linux as a base. Some anti cheat systems are considered invasive spyware, requiring high level permissions on a system, regardless if the player intends to engage in competitive play or not.

evidence_quotes.quote: Halo: The Master Chief Collection

paragraphs.text: Halo: The Master Chief Collection, published by Microsoft, allows players to launch the game without needing to install invasive third party anti-cheat code.

evidence_quotes.quote: more games being accessible on different systems

paragraphs.text: A positive economic impact from more games being accessible on different systems, allowing for competition in handheld computer hardware. Social impact allowing customers to have more confidence the games they are buying will be secure.
List of Linux games affected by anti-cheat
Harms of kernel level anti-cheat

evidence_quotes.quote: creating mods (user made modifications to games they have purchased)
players who have purchased the game want to create mods and then share them on unofficial 3rd party channels

paragraphs.text: Publishers should not be allowed to take action against people who, or install mechanisms that prevent people from, creating mods (user made modifications to games they have purchased). Publishers can moderate their own spaces, but if players who have purchased the game want to create mods and then share them on unofficial 3rd party channels, they should be allowed to do this as long as they adhere to copyright law and don’t distribute any copyrighted assets alongside mods. This is NOT to suggest a developer or publisher must offer modding support, which would be too onerous a requirement, rather that they should not interfere with modding if it is happening.

evidence_quotes.quote: many popular games first started as mods, such as ‘The Stanley Parable’, ‘PUBG’, amongst others.

paragraphs.text: This is a necessary part of modern creative process, and is important for the industry as many popular games first started as mods, such as ‘The Stanley Parable’, ‘PUBG’, amongst others.
Impact

evidence_quotes.quote: modders will be empowered to learn and develop skills using existing works and audiences as a base.

paragraphs.text: An social impact in that modders will be empowered to learn and develop skills using existing works and audiences as a base. An economic impact as the work modders make can itself be sold and spun off into commercial projects.
A list of games which started as mods

evidence_quotes.quote: the practice of modding

paragraphs.text: A publisher indicating they dislike the practice of modding and would want to interfere with consumer choices

evidence_quotes.quote: Publishers should not be allowed to use DRM after 12 weeks.
As DRM is anti-consumer, game publishers should be required to remove it 12 weeks after sale

paragraphs.text: Publishers should not be allowed to use DRM after 12 weeks. DRM helps sales initially and can protect against piracy, but research has shown that beyond 12 weeks DRM presence has no net benefit for publisher revenue. As DRM is anti-consumer, game publishers should be required to remove it 12 weeks after sale, and this strikes a balance allowing them to use it within the most profitable initial launch window.

evidence_quotes.quote: But then they keep using this DRM, negatively impacting legal players, long after launch.

items.topic: Cracked Versions Offer A Superior Experience

evidence_quotes.quote: giving illegal versions of the game a superior experience

paragraphs.text: A videogame launches using DRM to prevent piracy. But then they keep using this DRM, negatively impacting legal players, long after launch. Meanwhile an eventually “cracked” versions of the game could be pirated and played without negative side-effects, in effect giving illegal versions of the game a superior experience, possibly encouraging piracy.

evidence_quotes.quote: a “cracked” version of a game would no longer be of superior quality to a legally sold copy

paragraphs.text: This would have social impact, as DRM is often considered invasive software, and removing it would allow customers more confidence in the games they are buying. This would have positive economic impact, as a “cracked” version of a game would no longer be of superior quality to a legally sold copy, potentially driving sales.
Research conducted on the effect of DRM over time
E. Ban gambling with real money

evidence_quotes.quote: When games tie random outcomes to real world money spending, it should be banned entirely.
Otherwise, a game developer may include nudges to encourage gamers to engage with it anyway.

paragraphs.text: When games tie random outcomes to real world money spending, it should be banned entirely. This offers better protection for gamers than simply making it optional. Otherwise, a game developer may include nudges to encourage gamers to engage with it anyway.
Belgium banned entirely the concept of gambling based “loot boxes”, rather than make it an optional choice.

evidence_quotes.quote: not tricked into spending money

paragraphs.text: This would have massive social impact, protecting younger players who do not understand it and adults vulnerable to gambling addiction. It could also have an economic impact, as if people are not tricked into spending money, they will have more disposable income left to spend on other goods.
Belgium’s “loot box ban” [16]

evidence_quotes.quote: must allow purchasers of said files to download what they have purchased for their own use.

paragraphs.text: Storefronts that sell digital files (video games, music, comics, films, etc) must allow purchasers of said files to download what they have purchased for their own use. This ensures they will retain access to their purchases if they lose access to the account they purchased them from, or if the storefront removed the files for some reason.

evidence_quotes.quote: Many video game stores operate this way.
do not allow DRM-free downloads.

paragraphs.text: This has been standard operating procedure for the digital music industry since the introduction of MP3 sales. Many video game stores operate this way. However, some of the biggest video game stores do not, and many film and ebook stores do not allow DRM-free downloads.

evidence_quotes.quote: Allowing DRM-free downloads would have positive economic impacts, allowing for more competition as consumers could transfer their libraries from one ecosystem to another more easily.

paragraphs.text: Allowing DRM-free downloads would have positive economic impacts, allowing for more competition as consumers could transfer their libraries from one ecosystem to another more easily.
Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: to impose DRM on book products to further its own interests

paragraphs.text: Analysis of DRM-encumbered storefronts, described where amazon at first benefited from DRM-freeness by competing with iTunes, then later used it’s dominant market status to impose DRM on book products to further its own interests

evidence_quotes.quote: Personalised advertising should be prohibited outright

paragraphs.text: Personalised advertising should be prohibited outright in favour of contextual advertising, i.e. advertising targeted based on coarse location determined by IP address, or defined using keywords supplied by the publisher selling advertising space.

evidence_quotes.quote: targeted advertising

paragraphs.text: Advertising worked before Google and Facebook took over internet advertising by attaching it to user profiles. After they redefined the landscape to rely on targeted advertising, they as owners of the largest datasets and ad markets, were best placed to argue that this was the only way forward, and traditional contextual ads fell out of favour.

evidence_quotes.quote: levelling the playing field for new and traditional advertisers to compete with big data ad marketplaces
offering better deals for publishers selling ad space.

paragraphs.text: This would have social impact, aligning well with the GDPR. This would have economic benefits, levelling the playing field for new and traditional advertisers to compete with big data ad marketplaces, and offering better deals for publishers selling ad space.
Browser maker advocates against “surveillance based” advertising
Dutch website saw improvements after it stopped tracking

evidence_quotes.quote: do not wish for any cookies to be used beyond the most basic functional cookies required for the service to function.

paragraphs.text: If a user installs a signal in their browser that indicates they do not wish to use cookies or other storage and tracking systems, assume that the consumer is indicating they do not wish for any cookies to be used beyond the most basic functional cookies required for the service to function.
Example

evidence_quotes.quote: do not display a cookie banner

paragraphs.text: The “Do Not Track” header, proposed in 2009. If a DNT request is present, do not display a cookie banner, nor redirect to any other sites for cookie approval.
This would have social impact, meshing well with the EU GDPR and granting consumers choice.

evidence_quotes.quote: “Opt out preference signals”
“global privacy control”
targets the sale of data rather then opting out of tracking altogether

paragraphs.text: California has seen success by legally recognising such “Opt out preference signals”, however their implementation and “global privacy control” targets the sale of data rather then opting out of tracking altogether
No comment

evidence_quotes.quote: This is confusingly presented and often requires the ability to build or read a complex table in order to understand what is actually being purchased.
This can be used to push the consumer to buy more, or purchase extras like a “season pass” to ensure they do not miss out on anything (exploiting “FOMO”).

paragraphs.text: This is when one product will be sold in multiple different “editions” each with slightly different products included. This is confusingly presented and often requires the ability to build or read a complex table in order to understand what is actually being purchased. This can be used to push the consumer to buy more, or purchase extras like a “season pass” to ensure they do not miss out on anything (exploiting “FOMO”).

evidence_quotes.quote: At times different editions are constructed such that it is impossible to get everything without buying twice.
Often seen within the sale of videogames or bundles of digital files, with language like “standard”, “basic”, “legendary” editions.

paragraphs.text: Often seen within the sale of videogames or bundles of digital files, with language like “standard”, “basic”, “legendary” editions. It is unclear what needs to be purchased in order to get everything, and what content may be missing. At times different editions are constructed such that it is impossible to get everything without buying twice.

evidence_quotes.quote: Banning confusing editions, or finding some way to make them easier to navigate, would have social impact, helping consumers make more informed choices.

paragraphs.text: Banning confusing editions, or finding some way to make them easier to navigate, would have social impact, helping consumers make more informed choices.
Evidence
The game watch dogs 2 came with multiple confusing editions

evidence_quotes.quote: “lock away” access to certain features that are available at launch of a digital product, only to sell access to this as an extras.

paragraphs.text: Manufacturers should not be allowed to “lock away” access to certain features that are available at launch of a digital product, only to sell access to this as an extras. Manufacturers should sell complete products up front, and only offer sale of additional goods if they are developed and released some time after the initial launch window.

evidence_quotes.quote: Videogame Publishers take complete products and then parcel it up offering “day one DLC” in video games.
only for it to be locked until they pay even more to unlock it.
part of its use is restricted unless an extra payment or subscription is made.

paragraphs.text: Videogame Publishers take complete products and then parcel it up offering “day one DLC” in video games. At times complete content has been delivered to consumers, such as on a disc, only for it to be locked until they pay even more to unlock it. Alternatively, a physical product where part of its use is restricted unless an extra payment or subscription is made, like is increasingly happening in cars.

evidence_quotes.quote: without feeling pressured for buying incomplete products.

paragraphs.text: This would have social impact, empowering consumers to make better choices of how to spend money, without feeling pressured for buying incomplete products. This would also have economic benefits by allowing customers to better trust the brands they are buying from
Day one DLC can hurt a brand

evidence_quotes.quote: but unless you pay more, you end up with a vehicle where part of the battery range is unusable
unless you pay more

paragraphs.text: Tesla sells all vehicles with the same battery, but unless you pay more, you end up with a vehicle where part of the battery range is unusable

evidence_quotes.quote: For contracts bundling multiple services, it should be as easy to remove items from a contract as it is to add them.

paragraphs.text: For contracts bundling multiple services, it should be as easy to remove items from a contract as it is to add them.
Example

evidence_quotes.quote: to remove a single channel requires contacting customer support and waiting.

paragraphs.text: With a cable TV package it may be easy to add a channel with a few clicks in an account management website, but to remove a single channel requires contacting customer support and waiting.

This would have social impact, helping people be more in charge of their finances. It could also have economic impact, allowing customers to feel more confident in trying out new services without feeling locked in.
Evidence of this behaviour comes from anecdotal experience.

evidence_quotes.quote: to allow the consumer to decide exactly which services they want to subscribe to.
only to later increase the price once the user is locked in.

paragraphs.text: For contracts which bundle multiple services together, the seller must make available a detailed description of how these are built to allow the consumer to decide exactly which services they want to subscribe to. They may offer multiple services the user does not want at a cheaper price than the services would cost individually, only to later increase the price once the user is locked in.

evidence_quotes.quote: It may not be clear up front exactly what each individual component is worth.
Making the individual costs transparent would ensure that customer chosen bundles are not priced differently from seller-offered bundles.

paragraphs.text: A cloud provider may bundle storage gigabytes, media subscriptions, and other benefits all together. It may not be clear up front exactly what each individual component is worth. Customers should have the choice of explicitly stating exactly which components they want, letting them pick and choose their own bundles as well as the choice of seller-offered bundles. Making the individual costs transparent would ensure that customer chosen bundles are not priced differently from seller-offered bundles.
This would have social impact, helping customers feel more confident before entering into contracts.
Evidence of this behaviour comes from anecdotal experience.
Simplification
A. Copyright law

If some work has become a “copyright orphan”, where the copyright ownership of the original asset is unclear, it should be treated as existing in the public domain. This is particularly important for a digital economy where there may be no physical presence to investigate when identifying an owner, nor physical assets that can continue to be legally distributed. Where copyright owners have disappeared, ownership of copyrighted assets becomes unclear, and ideas have been locked out of use, this would allow the wider market to take advantage of those assets thus increasing competition and choice for consumers. It would also encourage copyright owners to better track and document ownership and reduce orphaned works in the future.
Example

evidence_quotes.quote: A popular old video game is made, and the publisher, developer, and platform owner are all defunct.

paragraphs.text: A popular old video game is made, and the publisher, developer, and platform owner are all defunct. No-one can use the copyrighted work anymore as it is unclear if anyone owns it. Permitting at the very least non-commercial use of this work would allow the cultural artefact to be restored via community driven emulation software.
Impact
This would bring positive economic impacts with new business opportunities in exploiting lost works. This would have social impact in the freeing up of lost cultural works.
Evidence
The EU has previously issued a directive attempting to resolve the problem of orphan works^{24}
However this does not seem to have solved the issue, notably with digital works like video games ^{25}
Horizontal issues
A. End of life plans for all technology

evidence_quotes.quote: designed such that it may stop operating without continued manufacturer or publisher support

paragraphs.text: When any technical system, be it a digital file, software, or hardware, is designed such that it may stop operating without continued manufacturer or publisher support, an end of life plan must be prepared. This ELP should come in the

form of sufficient documentation and data to explain to owners what steps to take to ensure they can continue to use the technology once support officially ends. Could also allow a community of users to organise their own continued support.

evidence_quotes.quote: Having an ELP which permits the medical service to patch the software
gives sufficient documentation allowing a community to repair any post-support failures

paragraphs.text: A state medical service offers internet connected prosthesis. When the manufacturer ends support, the prosthesis may cease functioning if it needs to connect to the manufacturer’s internet servers. Having an ELP which permits the medical service to patch the software, and gives sufficient documentation allowing a community to repair any post-support failures ensures longevity of the device.
This policy has massive impact for social care beyond simply consumer rights. There are environmental benefits by making devices more long lived.
See the impact of failure to offer an ELP for the Argus retinal prosthesis.
Connected home devices can become unusable after end of support

evidence_quotes.quote: Codify the right to interact with services through a custom interface, or to modify existing interfaces to suit the customers needs.
install custom software or firmware on any hardware a customer has purchased.
Allow consumers to use custom or third party hardware components with the hardware they have purchased.
Ban “parts pairing”.
Ensure that any device with a software component can be “unlocked” if the owner chooses.

paragraphs.text: Codify the right to interact with services through a custom interface, or to modify existing interfaces to suit the customers needs. Also ensure the right to install custom software or firmware on any hardware a customer has purchased. Allow consumers to use custom or third party hardware components with the hardware they have purchased. Ban “parts pairing”. Ensure that any device with a software component can be “unlocked” if the owner chooses.

section_path: B. Right to modification or use of third party components

evidence_quotes.quote: anti-consumer practices where software controls prevent cheaper or more competitive components from being used, such as banning third party printer ink cartridges

paragraphs.text: This would protect interactions such as the use accessibility software, and allow novel interactions to be developed such as applying a content filter on top of the service’s content to block unwanted or triggering content such as gambling. This could be done through 3rd party applications or operating systems, or by using extensions or modification of existing applications. This would also pre-empt anti-consumer practices where software controls prevent cheaper or more competitive components from being used, such as banning third party printer ink cartridges, or preventing agriculture vehicle repairs.

section_path: B. Right to modification or use of third party components

paragraphs.text: Impact

section_path: B. Right to modification or use of third party components

evidence_quotes.quote: Economic impact by allowing tertiary markets to sell custom modifications, components, or servicing for devices without needing to wait for gatekeeper approval.

paragraphs.text: This would have social impact, giving consumers more ownership of their devices and software, environmental impact by offering better longevity and maintainability of old devices. Economic impact by allowing tertiary markets to sell custom modifications, components, or servicing for devices without needing to wait for gatekeeper approval.

section_path: B. Right to modification or use of third party components

evidence_quotes.quote: block third party modifications or repairs to their devices

paragraphs.text: The US is moving against companies that block third party modifications or repairs to their devices such as john deere which cause high costs to farmers

section_path: B. Right to modification or use of third party components

evidence_quotes.quote: Payment processors should have a duty to carry and process payment to all digital sellers they have contracts with, without discriminating against what they are selling, unless a particular seller is explicitly embargoed by law.
Use legal powers to either ensure payment processors fulfil their obligations, or develop a European drop-in replacement that is more neutral and can be used as a last resort.

paragraphs.text: Payment processors should have a duty to carry and process payment to all digital sellers they have contracts with, without discriminating against what they are selling, unless a particular seller is explicitly embargoed by law. Use legal powers to either ensure payment processors fulfil their obligations, or develop a European drop-in replacement that is more neutral and can be used as a last resort.

evidence_quotes.quote: undue control over what kind of works can be sold
can terminate access at any time by making judgements over artistic disagreement.

paragraphs.text: Currently a very small number of payment processors and credit card companies have undue control over what kind of works can be sold, and can terminate access at any time by making judgements over artistic disagreement. Had that impacted an artists bottom line and forced them to stop work, they would have lost the ability to make a living expressing themselves freely. This behaviour harms consumer choice and harms market freedoms.

evidence_quotes.quote: taking payments themselves.

paragraphs.text: Economic benefits by guaranteeing that artists without large publisher backing can make a living taking payments themselves.
Impact of adult content bans pushed by payment processors on LGBTQ+ artists
Minecraft creator paypal account frozen
The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
